extra.reasoning:
The property (monotonicity in severe_headache) is VIOLATED. severe_headache appears in exactly one rule: r2 (priority 100, the highest), a 12-way conjunction that requires severe_headache plus all other symptoms to be true, and it sets acuity := self_care, the LOWEST acuity in the order [self_care, routine, urgent, emergent].

Baseline: all 12 other symptoms true and severe_headache = false. Here r2 does not fire (it needs severe_headache=true). Many priority-3 rules fire (r1 rash, r5 palpitations, r6 stiff_neck, r8 fever, r11 back_pain, r13 dyspnea), each setting acuity := emergent. The highest-priority matching rule among the firing rules is priority 3, so acuity = emergent.

Flip severe_headache to true (witness shown, all 13 inputs true): now the full conjunction of r2 is satisfied, so r2 fires at priority 100 — higher than every other rule's priority 3 or 2 — and wins, setting acuity := self_care.

Thus setting severe_headache true changes acuity from emergent (highest) to self_care (lowest), a strict decrease, which directly violates the monotonicity property. The witness_assignment is the input (with severe_headache=true) whose acuity (self_care) is lower than its severe_headache=false counterpart's acuity (emergent).
